tiny_practice.action: Pause and visualize light piercing through darkness, representing Christ's eternal presence

two_step.reflect_question: How can you allow Christ's light to shine through you today?

options.response: Intentionally offer encouragement to someone who seems discouraged
Look for a natural opportunity to share how Christ has transformed your life
Perform an anonymous act of service without seeking recognition

creative_prompt.instruction: Draw a picture representing 'light overcoming darkness', using only white and black colors

reevaluation.question: How has your understanding of Christ's divine nature changed after reading this chapter?
options: I see Jesus more as God than ever before
I understand His creative power more deeply
I recognize how He brings hope to darkness
I feel more connected to His eternal existence

three_choice.question: Which aspect of the Word's nature most intrigues you?

choices.text: The Word was with God and was God
choices.expansion: The mysterious co-eternal and co-equal nature of divine personhood challenges linear human understanding.

choices.text: All things were made through Him
choices.expansion: The creative agency of the Word reveals divine intentionality in universal design.

choices.text: The life was the light of men
choices.expansion: Light represents comprehension, revelation, and spiritual illumination beyond mere physical perception.

socratic_nudge.question: If the Word existed before creation, what does this suggest about the nature of existence itself?

moral_slider.question: How do you reconcile divine transcendence with personal relationship?

reevaluation.question: How has this passage reshaped your understanding of divine identity?
options: I see divinity as more complex
I'm more aware of symbolic spiritual language
I'm challenged to think beyond literal interpretations

thematic_summaries.title: The Eternal Word
thematic_summaries.summary: John introduces Jesus as the pre-existent divine Logos, existing before creation and being fully God. This passage reveals Christ's divine nature and his role in creation as an eternal, creative being.

thematic_summaries.title: Light and Darkness
thematic_summaries.summary: The metaphorical battle between light and darkness represents the spiritual conflict between divine truth and human ignorance. Christ's incarnation brings divine illumination into a world of spiritual darkness.

reevaluation.question: What does the 'Word' (Logos) reveal about Christ's nature and relationship to God?
options: Christ is a created being, subordinate to God
Christ is fully divine, co-eternal with the Father
Christ is a metaphorical representation of divine wisdom
Christ is a separate entity from God with distinct purposes
